description: M1 Pipeline Engine — Pipeline execution, stage sequencing, MCP agent spawning, Claude Code integration, stdio fallback, checkpoint/resume

userStories.title: Create AgentAdapter interface and types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a transport-agnostic interface for communicating with AI agents.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/agents/types.ts with AgentAdapter interface: sendMessage(prompt), getResponse(), abort(), status
Define AgentMessage type: { id: string, type: 'tool_call' | 'tool_result' | 'message', payload: unknown }
Define AgentStatus type: 'idle' | 'working' | 'error' | 'disconnected'
Define AgentConfig type: { name: string, model: string, provider: string, systemPrompt: string, transport: 'mcp' | 'stdio' | 'auto' }
Export all types from barrel file server/agents/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement stdio agent adapter for Claude Code
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to spawn Claude Code as a child process and communicate via JSON lines over stdin/stdout.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/agents/stdio-adapter.ts implementing AgentAdapter
Spawns `claude` as child process with `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag
Sends structured JSON lines over stdin
Reads structured JSON lines from stdout
Handles process exit and error events
abort() kills the child process
Export from server/agents/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the primary transport for Claude Code integration. MCP adapter is the alternative.

userStories.title: Implement MCP agent adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an MCP-based transport for agents that expose MCP servers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/agents/mcp-adapter.ts implementing AgentAdapter
Connects to an MCP server via `claude --mcp-server` or a custom MCP endpoint
Sends tool_call messages and receives tool_result responses
Implements the same AgentAdapter interface as stdio-adapter
Handles connection errors and reconnection
Export from server/agents/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add transport auto-detection and factory
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a factory that creates the right adapter based on config.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/agents/factory.ts with createAgent(config: AgentConfig): AgentAdapter
When transport is 'stdio', returns StdioAdapter
When transport is 'mcp', returns McpAdapter
When transport is 'auto', tries MCP first, falls back to stdio on failure
Config flag `claude_code.transport` is read from settings table or defaults to 'auto'
Export from server/agents/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create pipeline execution engine core
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a core engine that reads a pipeline definition and executes stages in order.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/pipeline/engine.ts with PipelineEngine class
Constructor takes a pipeline ID and loads stages from the database
run() method executes stages sequentially in sortOrder
Each stage: sets status to 'running', spawns agent via factory, waits for completion, sets status to 'done' or 'error'
Updates pipeline status in database as it progresses
Emits WebSocket events on stage status changes via broadcast()
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add checkpoint and resume to pipeline engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pipelines to checkpoint after each stage so they can resume from the last completed stage.
acceptanceCriteria: After each stage completes successfully, update pipeline.checkpointStageId to the completed stage ID
Add resume() method to PipelineEngine that starts from the stage after checkpointStageId
Skips stages with status 'done' when resuming
Pipeline status set to 'paused' when interrupted, 'running' when resumed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add parallel stage execution support
userStories.description: As a developer, I need stages with the same sortOrder to execute concurrently.
acceptanceCriteria: Stages with the same sortOrder value are grouped and executed in parallel via Promise.all
Each parallel stage gets its own agent instance
All parallel stages must complete before the next sortOrder group begins
If any parallel stage fails, remaining stages in the group continue but the pipeline reports partial failure
WebSocket events emitted for each parallel stage independently
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add pause, resume, and cancel controls to pipeline engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to pause, resume, and cancel pipelines at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Add pause() method: sets pipeline status to 'paused', waits for current stage to complete, stops execution
Add cancel() method: sets pipeline status to 'cancelled', aborts current agent, stops execution
resume() continues from checkpoint (already implemented in US-006)
All state changes persisted to database and broadcast via WebSocket
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create pipelines REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to create, list, and control pipelines.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/pipelines.ts as a Fastify plugin
GET /api/pipelines?projectId=X returns pipelines for a project
POST /api/pipelines creates a new pipeline with stages
POST /api/pipelines/:id/run starts pipeline execution
POST /api/pipelines/:id/pause pauses a running pipeline
POST /api/pipelines/:id/resume resumes a paused pipeline
POST /api/pipelines/:id/cancel cancels a pipeline
Register routes in server/index.ts
Input validation on all POST bodies
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create pipeline stages REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to manage individual stages within a pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to server/routes/pipelines.ts or create server/routes/stages.ts
GET /api/pipelines/:id/stages returns all stages for a pipeline in sortOrder
POST /api/pipelines/:id/stages adds a new stage to a pipeline
PUT /api/pipelines/:pipelineId/stages/:stageId updates a stage (name, agent, model, sortOrder)
DELETE /api/pipelines/:pipelineId/stages/:stageId removes a stage
POST /api/pipelines/:pipelineId/stages/:stageId/skip marks a stage as skipped
Input validation on all POST/PUT bodies
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create decisions REST API and WebSocket integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API for the decision queue so agents can ask questions and developers can respond.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/decisions.ts as a Fastify plugin
GET /api/decisions?projectId=X returns pending decisions
POST /api/decisions creates a new decision (called by pipeline engine when stage status is 'needs-you')
PUT /api/decisions/:id resolves a decision with the chosen option or free-text override
Resolving a decision broadcasts a WebSocket event to unblock the waiting agent
Register routes in server/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Connect Pipeline View to backend API
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Pipeline View to show real pipeline data from the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/pipelines.ts with usePipelines(projectId) and usePipelineStages(pipelineId) hooks
Update PipelineView.tsx to use API hooks instead of hardcoded PIPELINE_STAGES
Show loading and error states
Remove hardcoded PIPELINE_STAGES array
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add pipeline control buttons to Pipeline View
userStories.description: As a user, I want pause/resume/cancel buttons on the pipeline view.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a toolbar above pipeline stages with Pause, Resume, Cancel buttons
Buttons call the respective API endpoints (POST /api/pipelines/:id/pause etc.)
Buttons are conditionally enabled based on pipeline status (e.g., Pause only when running)
Status changes reflected in real-time via WebSocket subscription
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Connect Decision Queue in right sidebar to backend API
userStories.description: As a user, I want the decision queue to show real decisions and let me resolve them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/decisions.ts with useDecisions(projectId) and useResolveDecision() hooks
Update RightSidebar decision section to use API hooks instead of hardcoded data
Option buttons call PUT /api/decisions/:id with the selected option
'Let agent decide' button resolves with a special auto-decide flag
Decision queue updates in real-time via WebSocket
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add natural language steering bar functionality
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type natural language commands to steer the pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Steering input in PipelineView sends text to POST /api/pipelines/:id/steer endpoint
Create the /steer endpoint in server/routes/pipelines.ts
Endpoint accepts { message: string } body
For now, the endpoint parses simple commands: 'skip [stage]', 'add [stage name]', 'pause', 'resume', 'cancel'
Returns { action: string, result: string } response
Input clears after submission
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Full NLP steering will come later with AI provider integration (M2). For now, simple keyword parsing.

userStories.title: Add WebSocket subscription for real-time pipeline updates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the frontend to receive and display real-time pipeline and stage updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts that connects to ws://localhost:4321/ws
Hook reconnects automatically on disconnect with exponential backoff
Pipeline-related events ('pipeline:status', 'stage:status', 'decision:created') trigger TanStack Query cache invalidation
Active pipeline stages update in real-time without polling
Typecheck passes
